key_insight: A confined-space CVD strategy with tailored precursors and thermal ramps enables deterministic growth of high-quality Mo(W)Te2 single crystals, alloys, and atomically sharp lateral/vertical heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable route to reproducible Te-based heterointerfaces with controlled geometry and stacking for future quantum and topological devices.
method: Lowered the CVD growth temperature window using confined-space growth.
Used tailored precursor configurations plus stepwise thermal ramps to control phase and interface formation.
Applied atomic-resolution STEM and STM/STS to verify structure and electronic boundary quality.
evidence_signal: Aberration-corrected STEM showed nearly atomically sharp, compositionally well-defined Mo(W)Te2 boundaries, and STM/STS revealed distinct local density-of-states distributions across seamless lateral interfaces.
watch_item: Growth control may still depend sensitively on precursor setup, thermal ramp design, and the inherent polymorphism/instability of Mo(W)Te2 systems.
paper_title: Controlled chemical vapor deposition for synthesis of emerging Mo(W)Te2 systems
